title: Slow Cooked Canadian Sweet Potato Casserole
description: A floral, gooey, and cozy Canadian dish featuring sweet potatoes and a hint of spice, perfect for a cold winter night. This recipe is diabetic-friendly and features red pepper flakes for added flavor.
instructions: Preheat the slow cooker to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the cubed sweet potatoes, red pepper flakes, brown sugar, cinnamon, nutmeg, salt, and black pepper, and toss to coat.
Transfer the sweet potato mixture to the slow cooker and cook for 6 hours.
After 6 hours, remove the sweet potatoes from the slow cooker and immediately submerge them in an ice bath to stop the cooking process.
Once the sweet potatoes have cooled, transfer them to a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Top the sweet potatoes with the miniature marshmallows.
Return the dish to the slow cooker and cook for an additional 30 minutes, or until the marshmallows are toasted and golden brown.
Remove the dish from the slow cooker and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
